content.text: A tool call loop is the core execution pattern in every agentic application: send messages to Claude, execute any tool calls in the response, feed the results back, and repeat until the model reaches
loop handles both sequential and parallel tool calls identically — the difference is only in how many

attrs.alt: Tool call loop flowchart showing the agent execution cycle

content.text: The loop structure is simple by design — the model drives all decision-making. Your code only needs to execute what the model requests and feed the results back. The more interesting work is ensuring the loop terminates reliably and handles errors gracefully.
The simplest correct tool call loop in Python:
The two invariants this loop maintains: (1) every assistant response is appended before sending the next user message, (2) every
in a response gets a matching
. Break either invariant and the API returns a validation error.
Claude can return stop reasons beyond
. Handle all of them to avoid silent hangs:
A custom stop string was matched
For production servers (FastAPI, async workers), use the async Claude client so the loop does not block the event loop during API calls:
Each loop iteration appends two new messages (assistant + user). After many turns the history can exceed the model's context window or inflate token costs. Two strategies:
content with a short summary once the model has used that result (detected by the model issuing a subsequent call that references the data).
: Mark the system prompt and first few messages with
to avoid re-charging tokens for stable prefixes on every loop iteration.
: Calling the API again without appending the previous response gives the model no memory of what it just said — it repeats the same tool call forever.
Missing a tool_result for one of the tool_use blocks

with no corresponding result. Always process

everything that isn't already a string.

Claude supports server-sent event (SSE) streaming so users see text as it is generated rather than waiting for the full response. Streaming in a tool call loop requires reassembling the streamed chunks back into a complete response object before you can extract tool use blocks:
yields only text deltas (so users see output immediately), while
waits for the stream to close and returns the complete message including tool use blocks. During tool execution turns the text stream is empty (no text, only a

A system prompt scopes the agent's behaviour, restricts which tools it may use in context, and gives it any standing instructions. Pass it once at loop initialisation — it applies to every turn automatically:
System prompts incur token charges on every iteration. Use
on the system prompt to cache it across turns, dramatically reducing cost for long conversations.
Every response includes usage statistics. Tracking them across loop turns reveals how rapidly context grows and whether pruning is necessary:

Always append the full assistant response before sending the next user message — omitting it causes the model to lose track of its prior actions.
Guard against infinite loops with a
to run parallel tools and non-blocking API calls within the loop.
Prune large tool results from older turns to keep history within the context window and reduce per-call token costs.
